MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Carlos R. Jimenez, Esq., of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party T.W. Smith Company
Stephen J. Healy, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Garry Easley

RULING:
DEFENDANT T.W. SMITH COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF PERCIPIENT WITNESS PHILLIP SHAW AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AT DEPOSITION; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant T.W. Smith Company's motion to compel the deposition of Phillip Shaw is denied. While it is commendable that defendant has sought to work with plaintiff's counsel to arrange for a convenient date for the deposition of Mr. Shaw, absent a showing that Mr. Shaw is represented by plaintiff's counsel or that Mr. Shaw has agreed to abide by scheduling arranged by plaintiff's counsel, plaintiff has no legal obligation to make Mr. Shaw available for a deposition. Defendant's remedy is subpoena Mr. Shaw. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
